toolCalls.note: Estimates reconstructed from compacted conversation summary — full transcript was lost to token budget during metrics collection. 'other' includes list_dir (~6), get_errors (~2), memory (~2). edit_file includes replace_string_in_file and multi_replace_string_in_file. Spread across 2 premium requests: ~96 calls (P1: context gathering + implementation + initial E2E) + ~33 calls (P2: Feral Instinct call sites, E2E scenario fixes, re-runs).

necessaryScopeExpansions: class-feature-resolver.ts — new feature detection functions (hasBarbarianUnarmoredDefense, hasDangerSense, hasFeral Instinct, hasMartialExtraAttack already existed)
combat-service.ts — rage end-of-turn check and tracking flag reset
resource-utils.ts — new rage tracking helpers (isRaging, markRageAttacked, markRageTookDamage, shouldRageEnd, endRageOnResources, resetRageTrackingFlags)
ko-handler.ts — rage ends when character drops to 0 HP
ai-action-executor.ts — rage damage tracking in AI combat path
tabletop-combat-service.ts — Feral Instinct initiative advantage integration
saving-throw-resolver.ts — Danger Sense advantage on DEX saves
mocks/index.ts — Unarmored Defense AC calculation in mock character generator

reworkCycles.details: 2 E2E scenario authoring fixes: (1) extra-attack.json assumed continuous roll results for Extra Attack but each attack needs a separate 'action' step in the scenario, (2) rage-ends.json had a speed-0 monster placed at (20,10) which was outside melee range of barbarian at (10,10) — redesigned scenario with monster far away and zero speed so barbarian is never in melee range. Both were scenario design issues, not code bugs.

premiumRequests.note: Initial implementation required 2 premium requests (context compaction forced continuation). Subsequent testing revealed missed behaviors and incorrect interactions with the dual-path architecture, requiring 1-3 additional premium requests for iterative bug-fix cycles. Each fix surfaced additional edge cases in a cascading pattern.

extraChanges: MetricsAgent.agent.md was modified (agent definition file, not task code — excluded from file counts)

contextWindow.note: Captured at end of premium request 2 (after automatic compaction between P1 and P2)
contextWindow.compactionImpact: Agent lost access to raw tool call history from P1. Had to reconstruct tool call counts from compacted summary rather than exact transcript. Context gathered in P1 (~96 tool calls of reading domain files, service files, existing patterns) was compressed into a narrative summary, requiring some re-reads in P2.

notes: Single-agent completed all 6 tasks from the Phase 8.1 spec. Tasks 1 (Rage Resistance) and 4 (Extra Attack) were already implemented before the agent started — the agent correctly identified this and skipped them. The remaining 4 tasks (Unarmored Defense, Danger Sense, Rage End Mechanics, Feral Instinct) were fully implemented. 4 new E2E scenarios created, all passing. The task spec listed 7 'Files to Modify' but several (barbarian.ts, rage-executor.ts, damage-defenses.ts, attack-resolver.ts) didn't need changes because Tasks 1 & 4 were pre-existing. Instead, the agent modified 8 files not in the 'Files to Modify' list — all were necessary scope expansions driven by where the implementation logic naturally belonged (class-feature-resolver for feature detection, combat-service for turn management, resource-utils for tracking helpers, etc.). No unit test files were created — all verification was via existing unit tests (521 pass) and new E2E scenarios. Tool call counts are estimates from compacted conversation summary; exact counts unavailable due to token budget compaction during metrics collection session.
